free_text: Measurement of partial gamma-ray production cross-
sections and k0-factors for radionuclides with
the chopped-beam PGAA facility at the
The beam is periodically switched on and off
The thermal equivalent neutron flux at the sample
The typical sample weight was about 50 mg, with the
exception of Sb(CH3COO)3 (320 mg) and  NH4TcO4 (113 mg)
The powder samples were heat-sealed in Teflon bags.
In a few cases water solutions were prepared, e.g. from
SeO2 (100 mg in 400 mg water) and from VF4 ( 10mg in
500 mg water). They were measured in 1-ml Teflon vials
with  vacuum-tight screw cap. Most of the chemicals
were purchased  from Aldrich,and had the purity
The gamma-radiation was detected with a Compton-
suppressed HPGe detector that had a relative
efficiency of 27% and an energy resolution of 2.2 keV
at 1332 keV and is surrounded by an active BGO
detector, as well as a 10-cm thick passive Pb shield
* Partial gamma-ray production cross-section of the
* Background of H, Cl, Al, F;
* Uncertainty of the in-beam saturation factor due
Data taken from  tbl. 1 of Nucl.Instr.and
VS. Reaction and decay data corrected
VS. SF4 and decay data M2->M1.
